verses.kjv: And Zechariah the son of Meshelemiah was porter of the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
verses.bsb: Zechariah son of Meshelemiah was the gatekeeper at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.

verses.kjv: All these which were chosen to be porters in the gates were two hundred and twelve. These were reckoned by their genealogy in their villages, whom David and Samuel the seer did ordain in their set office.
verses.bsb: The number of those chosen to be gatekeepers at the thresholds was 212. They were registered by genealogy in their villages. David and Samuel the seer had appointed them to their positions of trust.

verses.kjv: So they and their children had the oversight of the gates of the house of the Lord, namely, the house of the tabernacle, by wards.
verses.bsb: So they and their descendants were assigned to guard the gates of the house of the LORD—the house called the Tent.

verses.kjv: In four quarters were the porters, toward the east, west, north, and south.
verses.bsb: The gatekeepers were stationed on the four sides: east, west, north, and south.

verses.kjv: And their brethren, which were in their villages, were to come after seven days from time to time with them.
verses.bsb: Their relatives came from their villages at fixed times to serve with them for seven-day periods.

verses.kjv: For these Levites, the four chief porters, were in their set office, and were over the chambers and treasuries of the house of God.
verses.bsb: But the four chief gatekeepers, who were Levites, were entrusted with the rooms and the treasuries of the house of God.

verses.kjv: ¶ And they lodged round about the house of God, because the charge was upon them, and the opening thereof every morning pertained to them.
verses.bsb: They would spend the night stationed around the house of God, because they were responsible for guarding it and opening it every morning.

verses.kjv: And certain of them had the charge of the ministering vessels, that they should bring them in and out by tale.
verses.bsb: Some of them were in charge of the articles used in worship, to count them whenever they were brought in or taken out.

verses.kjv: Some of them also were appointed to oversee the vessels, and all the instruments of the sanctuary, and the fine flour, and the wine, and the oil, and the frankincense, and the spices.
verses.bsb: Others were put in charge of the furnishings and other articles of the sanctuary, as well as the fine flour, wine, oil, frankincense, and spices.

verses.kjv: And some of the sons of the priests made the ointment of the spices.
verses.bsb: And some of the sons of the priests mixed the spices.

verses.kjv: And Mattithiah, one of the Levites, who was the firstborn of Shallum the Korahite, had the set office over the things that were made in the pans.
verses.bsb: A Levite named Mattithiah, the firstborn son of Shallum the Korahite, was entrusted with baking the bread.

verses.kjv: And other of their brethren, of the sons of the Kohathites, were over the shewbread, to prepare it every sabbath.
verses.bsb: Some of their Kohathite brothers were responsible for preparing the rows of the showbread every Sabbath.

verses.kjv: And these are the singers, chief of the fathers of the Levites, who remaining in the chambers were free: for they were employed in that work day and night.
verses.bsb: Those who were musicians, the heads of Levite families, stayed in the temple chambers and were exempt from other duties because they were on duty day and night.

verses.kjv: These chief fathers of the Levites were chief throughout their generations; these dwelt at Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: All these were heads of Levite families, chiefs according to their genealogies, and they lived in Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: ¶ And in Gibeon dwelt the father of Gibeon, Jehiel, whose wife’s name was Maachah:
verses.bsb: Jeiel the father of Gibeon lived in Gibeon. His wife’s name was Maacah.

verses.kjv: And his firstborn son Abdon, then Zur, and Kish, and Baal, and Ner, and Nadab,
verses.bsb: Abdon was his firstborn son, then Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, Nadab,

verses.kjv: And Gedor, and Ahio, and Zechariah, and Mikloth.
verses.bsb: Gedor, Ahio, Zechariah, and Mikloth.

verses.kjv: And Mikloth begat Shimeam. And they also dwelt with their brethren at Jerusalem, over against their brethren.
verses.bsb: Mikloth was the father of Shimeam. They too lived alongside their relatives in Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: And Ner begat Kish; and Kish begat Saul; and Saul begat Jonathan, and Malchi-shua, and Abinadab, and Esh-baal.
verses.bsb: Ner was the father of Kish, Kish was the father of Saul, and Saul was the father of Jonathan, Malchishua, Abinadab, and Esh-baal.

verses.kjv: And the son of Jonathan was Merib-baal: and Merib-baal begat Micah.
verses.bsb: The son of Jonathan:   Merib-baal, who was the father of Micah.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Micah were, Pithon, and Melech, and Tahrea, and Ahaz.
verses.bsb: The sons of Micah:   Pithon, Melech, Tahrea, and Ahaz.

verses.kjv: And Ahaz begat Jarah; and Jarah begat Alemeth, and Azmaveth, and Zimri; and Zimri begat Moza;
verses.bsb: Ahaz was the father of Jarah; Jarah was the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri; and Zimri was the father of Moza.

verses.kjv: And Moza begat Binea; and Rephaiah his son, Eleasah his son, Azel his son.
verses.bsb: Moza was the father of Binea. Rephaiah was his son, Elasah his son, and Azel his son.

verses.kjv: And Azel had six sons, whose names are these, Azrikam, Bocheru, and Ishmael, and Sheariah, and Obadiah, and Hanan: these were the sons of Azel.
verses.bsb: And Azel had six sons, and these were their names:   Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan. These were the sons of Azel.

verses.kjv: NOW the Philistines fought against Israel; and the men of Israel fled from before the Philistines, and fell down slain in mount Gilboa.
verses.bsb: Now the Philistines fought against Israel, and the men of Israel fled before them, and many fell slain on Mount Gilboa.

verses.kjv: And the Philistines followed hard after Saul, and after his sons; and the Philistines slew Jonathan, and Abinadab, and Malchi-shua, the sons of Saul.
verses.bsb: The Philistines followed hard after Saul and his sons, and they killed Saul’s sons Jonathan, Abinadab, and Malchishua.

verses.kjv: And the battle went sore against Saul, and the archers hit him, and he was wounded of the archers.
verses.bsb: When the battle intensified against Saul, the archers overtook him and wounded him.

verses.kjv: Then said Saul to his armourbearer, Draw thy sword, and thrust me through therewith; lest these uncircumcised come and abuse me. But his armourbearer would not; for he was sore afraid. So Saul took a sword, and fell upon it.
verses.bsb: Then Saul said to his armor-bearer, “Draw your sword and run it through me, or these uncircumcised men will come and torture me!”  But his armor-bearer was terrified and refused to do it. So Saul took his own sword and fell on it.

verses.kjv: And when his armourbearer saw that Saul was dead, he fell likewise on the sword, and died.
verses.bsb: When his armor-bearer saw that Saul was dead, he too fell on his own sword and died.

verses.kjv: So Saul died, and his three sons, and all his house died together.
verses.bsb: So Saul died together with his three sons and all his house.

verses.kjv: And when all the men of Israel that were in the valley saw that they fled, and that Saul and his sons were dead, then they forsook their cities, and fled: and the Philistines came and dwelt in them.
verses.bsb: When all the Israelites in the valley saw that the army had fled and that Saul and his sons had died, they abandoned their cities and ran away. So the Philistines came and occupied their cities.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass on the morrow, when the Philistines came to strip the slain, that they found Saul and his sons fallen in mount Gilboa.
verses.bsb: The next day, when the Philistines came to strip the dead, they found Saul and his sons fallen on Mount Gilboa.

verses.kjv: And when they had stripped him, they took his head, and his armour, and sent into the land of the Philistines round about, to carry tidings unto their idols, and to the people.
verses.bsb: They stripped Saul, cut off his head, took his armor, and sent messengers throughout the land of the Philistines to proclaim the news in the temple of their idols and among their people.

verses.kjv: And they put his armour in the house of their gods, and fastened his head in the temple of Dagon.
verses.bsb: They put his armor in the temple of their gods and hung his head in the temple of Dagon.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when all Jabesh-gilead heard all that the Philistines had done to Saul,
verses.bsb: When the people of Jabesh-gilead heard about everything the Philistines had done to Saul,

verses.kjv: They arose, all the valiant men, and took away the body of Saul, and the bodies of his sons, and brought them to Jabesh, and buried their bones under the oak in Jabesh, and fasted seven days.
verses.bsb: all their men of valor set out and retrieved the bodies of Saul and his sons and brought them to Jabesh. And they buried their bones under the oak in Jabesh and fasted seven days.

verses.kjv: ¶ So Saul died for his transgression which he committed against the Lord, even against the word of the Lord, which he kept not, and also for asking counsel of one that had a familiar spirit, to inquire of it;
verses.bsb: So Saul died for his unfaithfulness to the LORD, because he did not keep the word of the LORD and even consulted a medium for guidance,

verses.kjv: And inquired not of the Lord: therefore he slew him, and turned the kingdom unto David the son of Jesse.
verses.bsb: and he failed to inquire of the LORD. So the LORD put him to death and turned the kingdom over to David son of Jesse.

verses.kjv: THEN all Israel gathered themselves to David unto Hebron, saying, Behold, we are thy bone and thy flesh.
verses.bsb: Then all Israel came together to David at Hebron and said, “Here we are, your own flesh and blood.

verses.kjv: And moreover in time past, even when Saul was king, thou wast he that leddest out and broughtest in Israel: and the Lord thy God said unto thee, Thou shalt feed my people Israel, and thou shalt be ruler over my people Israel.
verses.bsb: Even in times past, while Saul was king, you were the one who led Israel out and brought them back. And the LORD your God said, ‘You will shepherd My people Israel, and you will be ruler over them.’”

verses.kjv: Therefore came all the elders of Israel to the king to Hebron; and David made a covenant with them in Hebron before the Lord; and they anointed David king over Israel, according to the word of the Lord by Samuel.
verses.bsb: So all the elders of Israel came to the king at Hebron, where David made a covenant with them before the LORD. And they anointed him king over Israel, according to the word of the LORD through Samuel.

verses.kjv: ¶ And David and all Israel went to Jerusalem, which is Jebus; where the Jebusites were, the inhabitants of the land.
verses.bsb: Then David and all the Israelites marched to Jerusalem (that is, Jebus), where the Jebusites inhabited the land.

verses.kjv: And the inhabitants of Jebus said to David, Thou shalt not come hither. Nevertheless David took the castle of Zion, which is the city of David.
verses.bsb: The people of Jebus said to David, “You will never get in here.”  Nevertheless, David captured the fortress of Zion (that is, the City of David).

verses.kjv: And David said, Whosoever smiteth the Jebusites first shall be chief and captain. So Joab the son of Zeruiah went first up, and was chief.
verses.bsb: Now David had said, “Whoever is the first to strike down a Jebusite will become chief commander.”  And Joab son of Zeruiah went up first, and he became the chief.

verses.kjv: And David dwelt in the castle; therefore they called it the city of David.
verses.bsb: So David took up residence in the fortress; that is why it was called the City of David.

verses.kjv: And he built the city round about, even from Millo round about: and Joab repaired the rest of the city.
verses.bsb: He built up the city around it, from the supporting terraces to the surrounding wall, while Joab restored the rest of the city.

verses.kjv: So David waxed greater and greater: for the Lord of hosts was with him.
verses.bsb: And David became greater and greater, for the LORD of Hosts was with him.

verses.kjv: ¶ These also are the chief of the mighty men whom David had, who strengthened themselves with him in his kingdom, and with all Israel, to make him king, according to the word of the Lord concerning Israel.
verses.bsb: Now these were the chiefs of David’s mighty men, who, together with all Israel, bolstered and strengthened his kingdom, according to the word of the LORD concerning Israel.

verses.kjv: And this is the number of the mighty men whom David had; Jashobeam, an Hachmonite, the chief of the captains: he lifted up his spear against three hundred slain by him at one time.
verses.bsb: This is the list of David’s mighty men:  Jashobeam son of Hachmoni was chief of the officers; he wielded his spear against three hundred men, whom he killed at one time.

verses.kjv: And after him was Eleazar the son of Dodo, the Ahohite, who was one of the three mighties.
verses.bsb: Next in command was Eleazar son of Dodo the Ahohite, one of the three mighty men.

verses.kjv: He was with David at Pas-dammim, and there the Philistines were gathered together to battle, where was a parcel of ground full of barley; and the people fled from before the Philistines.
verses.bsb: He was with David at Pas-dammim when the Philistines gathered there for battle. At the place with a field full of barley, the troops fled from the Philistines.

verses.kjv: And they set themselves in the midst of that parcel, and delivered it, and slew the Philistines; and the Lord saved them by a great deliverance.
verses.bsb: But Eleazar and David stationed themselves in the middle of the field and defended it. They struck down the Philistines, and the LORD brought about a great victory.

verses.kjv: ¶ Now three of the thirty captains went down to the rock to David, into the cave of Adullam; and the host of the Philistines encamped in the valley of Rephaim.
verses.bsb: Three of the thirty chief men went down to David, to the rock at the cave of Adullam, while a company of Philistines was encamped in the Valley of Rephaim.

verses.kjv: And David was then in the hold, and the Philistines’ garrison was then at Beth-lehem.
verses.bsb: At that time David was in the stronghold, and the garrison of the Philistines was at Bethlehem.

verses.kjv: And David longed, and said, Oh that one would give me drink of the water of the well of Beth-lehem, that is at the gate!
verses.bsb: David longed for water and said, “Oh, that someone would get me a drink of water from the well near the gate of Bethlehem!”

verses.kjv: And the three brake through the host of the Philistines, and drew water out of the well of Beth-lehem, that was by the gate, and took it, and brought it to David: but David would not drink of it, but poured it out to the Lord,
verses.bsb: So the Three broke through the Philistine camp, drew water from the well at the gate of Bethlehem, and brought it back to David. But he refused to drink it; instead, he poured it out to the LORD,

verses.kjv: And said, My God forbid it me, that I should do this thing: shall I drink the blood of these men that have put their lives in jeopardy? for with the jeopardy of their lives they brought it. Therefore he would not drink it. These things did these three mightiest.
verses.bsb: saying, “Far be it from me, my God, to do this! How can I drink the blood of these men who risked their lives?” Because they had brought it at the risk of their lives, David refused to drink it.  Such were the exploits of the three mighty men.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Abishai the brother of Joab, he was chief of the three: for lifting up his spear against three hundred, he slew them, and had a name among the three.
verses.bsb: Now Abishai, the brother of Joab, was chief of the Three, and he lifted his spear against three hundred men, killed them, and won a name along with the Three.

verses.kjv: Of the three, he was more honourable than the two; for he was their captain: howbeit he attained not to the first three.
verses.bsb: He was doubly honored above the Three, and he became their commander, even though he was not included among the Three.

verses.kjv: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, the son of a valiant man of Kabzeel, who had done many acts; he slew two lionlike men of Moab: also he went down and slew a lion in a pit in a snowy day.
verses.bsb: And Benaiah son of Jehoiada was a man of valor from Kabzeel, a man of many exploits. He struck down two champions of Moab, and on a snowy day he went down into a pit and killed a lion.

verses.kjv: And he slew an Egyptian, a man of great stature, five cubits high; and in the Egyptian’s hand was a spear like a weaver’s beam; and he went down to him with a staff, and plucked the spear out of the Egyptian’s hand, and slew him with his own spear.
verses.bsb: He also killed an Egyptian, a huge man five cubits tall. Although the Egyptian had a spear like a weaver’s beam in his hand, Benaiah went against him with a club, snatched the spear from his hand, and killed the Egyptian with his own spear.

verses.kjv: These things did Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and had the name among the three mighties.
verses.bsb: These were the exploits of Benaiah son of Jehoiada, who won a name alongside the three mighty men.

verses.kjv: Behold, he was honourable among the thirty, but attained not to the first three: and David set him over his guard.
verses.bsb: He was most honored among the Thirty, but he did not become one of the Three. And David appointed him over his guard.

verses.kjv: ¶ Also the valiant men of the armies were, Asahel the brother of Joab, Elhanan the son of Dodo of Beth-lehem,
verses.bsb: Now these were the mighty men:  Asahel the brother of Joab,   Elhanan son of Dodo of Bethlehem,

verses.kjv: Shammoth the Harorite, Helez the Pelonite,
verses.bsb: Shammoth the Harorite,   Helez the Pelonite,

verses.kjv: Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite, Abi-ezer the Antothite,
verses.bsb: Ira son of Ikkesh the Tekoite,   Abiezer the Anathothite,

verses.kjv: Sibbecai the Hushathite, Ilai the Ahohite,
verses.bsb: Sibbecai the Hushathite,   Ilai the Ahohite,

verses.kjv: Maharai the Netophathite, Heled the son of Baanah the Netophathite,
verses.bsb: Maharai the Netophathite,   Heled son of Baanah the Netophathite,

verses.kjv: Ithai the son of Ribai of Gibeah, that pertained to the children of Benjamin, Benaiah the Pirathonite,
verses.bsb: Ithai son of Ribai from Gibeah of the Benjamites,   Benaiah the Pirathonite,

verses.kjv: Hurai of the brooks of Gaash, Abiel the Arbathite,
verses.bsb: Hurai from the brooks of Gaash,   Abiel the Arbathite,

verses.kjv: Azmaveth the Baharumite, Eliahba the Shaalbonite,
verses.bsb: Azmaveth the Baharumite,   Eliahba the Shaalbonite,

verses.kjv: The sons of Hashem the Gizonite, Jonathan the son of Shage the Hararite,
verses.bsb: the sons of Hashem the Gizonite,   Jonathan son of Shagee the Hararite,

verses.kjv: Ahiam the son of Sacar the Hararite, Eliphal the son of Ur,
verses.bsb: Ahiam son of Sachar the Hararite,   Eliphal son of Ur,

verses.kjv: Hepher the Mecherathite, Ahijah the Pelonite,
verses.bsb: Hepher the Mecherathite,   Ahijah the Pelonite,

verses.kjv: Hezro the Carmelite, Naarai the son of Ezbai,
verses.bsb: Hezro the Carmelite,   Naarai son of Ezbai,

verses.kjv: Joel the brother of Nathan, Mibhar the son of Haggeri,
verses.bsb: Joel the brother of Nathan,   Mibhar son of Hagri,

verses.kjv: Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai the Berothite, the armourbearer of Joab the son of Zeruiah,
verses.bsb: Zelek the Ammonite,   Naharai the Beerothite, the armor-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah,

verses.kjv: Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite,
verses.bsb: Ira the Ithrite,   Gareb the Ithrite,

verses.kjv: Uriah the Hittite, Zabad the son of Ahlai,
verses.bsb: Uriah the Hittite,   Zabad son of Ahlai,

verses.kjv: Adina the son of Shiza the Reubenite, a captain of the Reubenites, and thirty with him,
verses.bsb: Adina son of Shiza the Reubenite, chief of the Reubenites, and the thirty with him,

verses.kjv: Hanan the son of Maachah, and Joshaphat the Mithnite,
verses.bsb: Hanan son of Maacah,   Joshaphat the Mithnite,

verses.kjv: Uzzia the Ashterathite, Shama and Jehiel the sons of Hothan the Aroerite,
verses.bsb: Uzzia the Ashterathite,   Shama and Jeiel the sons of Hotham the Aroerite,

verses.kjv: Jediael the son of Shimri, and Joha his brother, the Tizite,
verses.bsb: Jediael son of Shimri and his brother Joha the Tizite,

verses.kjv: Eliel the Mahavite, and Jeribai, and Joshaviah, the sons of Elnaam, and Ithmah the Moabite,
verses.bsb: Eliel the Mahavite,   Jeribai and Joshaviah, the sons of Elnaam,   Ithmah the Moabite,

verses.kjv: Eliel, and Obed, and Jasiel the Mesobaite.
verses.bsb: Eliel, Obed, and Jaasiel the Mezobaite.

verses.kjv: NOW these are they that came to David to Ziklag, while he yet kept himself close because of Saul the son of Kish: and they were among the mighty men, helpers of the war.
verses.bsb: Now these were the men who came to David at Ziklag, while he was still banished from the presence of Saul son of Kish (they were among the mighty men who helped him in battle;

verses.kjv: They were armed with bows, and could use both the right hand and the left in hurling stones and shooting arrows out of a bow, even of Saul’s brethren of Benjamin.
verses.bsb: they were archers using both the right and left hands to sling stones and shoot arrows; and they were Saul’s kinsmen from Benjamin):

verses.kjv: The chief was Ahiezer, then Joash, the sons of Shemaah the Gibeathite; and Jeziel, and Pelet, the sons of Azmaveth; and Berachah, and Jehu the Antothite,
verses.bsb: Ahiezer their chief and Joash, who were the sons of Shemaah the Gibeathite;   Jeziel and Pelet, the sons of Azmaveth;   Beracah;   Jehu the Anathothite;

verses.kjv: And Ismaiah the Gibeonite, a mighty man among the thirty, and over the thirty; and Jeremiah, and Jahaziel, and Johanan, and Josabad the Gederathite,
verses.bsb: Ishmaiah the Gibeonite, a mighty man among the Thirty and a leader over the Thirty;   Jeremiah, Jahaziel, Johanan, and Jozabad the Gederathite;

verses.kjv: Eluzai, and Jerimoth, and Bealiah, and Shemariah, and Shephatiah the Haruphite,
verses.bsb: Eluzai, Jerimoth, Bealiah, Shemariah, and Shephatiah the Haruphite;

verses.kjv: Elkanah, and Jesiah, and Azareel, and Joezer, and Jashobeam, the Korhites,
verses.bsb: Elkanah, Isshiah, Azarel, Joezer, and Jashobeam, who were Korahites;

verses.kjv: And Joelah, and Zebadiah, the sons of Jeroham of Gedor.
verses.bsb: and Joelah and Zebadiah, the sons of Jeroham from Gedor.

verses.kjv: And of the Gadites there separated themselves unto David into the hold to the wilderness men of might, and men of war fit for the battle, that could handle shield and buckler, whose faces were like the faces of lions, and were as swift as the roes upon the mountains;
verses.bsb: Some Gadites defected to David at his stronghold in the desert. They were mighty men of valor, trained for battle, experts with the shield and spear, whose faces were like the faces of lions and who were as swift as gazelles on the mountains:

verses.kjv: Ezer the first, Obadiah the second, Eliab the third,
verses.bsb: Ezer the chief, Obadiah the second in command, Eliab the third,

verses.kjv: Mishmannah the fourth, Jeremiah the fifth,
verses.bsb: Mishmannah the fourth, Jeremiah the fifth,

verses.kjv: Attai the sixth, Eliel the seventh,
verses.bsb: Attai the sixth, Eliel the seventh,

verses.kjv: Johanan the eighth, Elzabad the ninth,
verses.bsb: Johanan the eighth, Elzabad the ninth,

verses.kjv: Jeremiah the tenth, Machbanai the eleventh.
verses.bsb: Jeremiah the tenth, and Machbanai the eleventh.

verses.kjv: These were of the sons of Gad, captains of the host: one of the least was over an hundred, and the greatest over a thousand.
verses.bsb: These Gadites were army commanders, the least of whom was a match for a hundred, and the greatest for a thousand.

verses.kjv: These are they that went over Jordan in the first month, when it had overflown all his banks; and they put to flight all them of the valleys, both toward the east, and toward the west.
verses.bsb: These are the ones who crossed the Jordan in the first month when it was overflowing all its banks, and they put to flight all those in the valleys, both to the east and to the west.

verses.kjv: And there came of the children of Benjamin and Judah to the hold unto David.
verses.bsb: Other Benjamites and some men from Judah also came to David in his stronghold.

verses.kjv: And David went out to meet them, and answered and said unto them, If ye be come peaceably unto me to help me, mine heart shall be knit unto you: but if ye be come to betray me to mine enemies, seeing there is no wrong in mine hands, the God of our fathers look thereon, and rebuke it.
verses.bsb: And David went out to meet them, saying, “If you have come to me in peace to help me, my heart will be united with you; but if you have come to betray me to my enemies when my hands are free of violence, may the God of our fathers see it and judge you.”

verses.kjv: Then the spirit came upon Amasai, who was chief of the captains, and he said, Thine are we, David, and on thy side, thou son of Jesse: peace, peace be unto thee, and peace be to thine helpers; for thy God helpeth thee. Then David received them, and made them captains of the band.
verses.bsb: Then the Spirit came upon Amasai, the chief of the Thirty, and he said:  “We are yours, O David! We are with you, O son of Jesse! Peace, peace to you, and peace to your helpers, for your God helps you.”   So David received them and made them leaders of his troops.

verses.kjv: And there fell some of Manasseh to David, when he came with the Philistines against Saul to battle: but they helped them not: for the lords of the Philistines upon advisement sent him away, saying, He will fall to his master Saul to the jeopardy of our heads.
verses.bsb: Some from Manasseh defected to David when he went with the Philistines to fight against Saul. (They did not help the Philistines because the Philistine rulers consulted and sent David away, saying, “It will cost us our heads if he defects to his master Saul.”)

verses.kjv: As he went to Ziklag, there fell to him of Manasseh, Adnah, and Jozabad, and Jediael, and Michael, and Jozabad, and Elihu, and Zilthai, captains of the thousands that were of Manasseh.
verses.bsb: When David went to Ziklag, these men of Manasseh defected to him:  Adnah, Jozabad, Jediael, Michael, Jozabad, Elihu, and Zillethai, chiefs of thousands in Manasseh.

verses.kjv: And they helped David against the band of the rovers: for they were all mighty men of valour, and were captains in the host.
verses.bsb: They helped David against the raiders, for they were all mighty men of valor and commanders in the army.

verses.kjv: For at that time day by day there came to David to help him, until it was a great host, like the host of God.
verses.bsb: For at that time men came to David day after day to help him, until he had a great army, like the army of God.

verses.kjv: ¶ And these are the numbers of the bands that were ready armed to the war, and came to David to Hebron, to turn the kingdom of Saul to him, according to the word of the Lord.
verses.bsb: Now these are the numbers of men armed for battle who came to David at Hebron to turn Saul’s kingdom over to him, in accordance with the word of the LORD:

verses.kjv: The children of Judah that bare shield and spear were six thousand and eight hundred, ready armed to the war.
verses.bsb: From Judah: 6,800 armed troops bearing shields and spears.

verses.kjv: Of the children of Simeon, mighty men of valour for the war, seven thousand and one hundred.
verses.bsb: From Simeon: 7,100 mighty men of valor, ready for battle.

verses.kjv: Of the children of Levi four thousand and six hundred.
verses.bsb: From Levi: 4,600,

verses.kjv: And Jehoiada was the leader of the Aaronites, and with him were three thousand and seven hundred;
verses.bsb: including Jehoiada, leader of the house of Aaron, with 3,700 men,

verses.kjv: And Zadok, a young man mighty of valour, and of his father’s house twenty and two captains.
verses.bsb: and Zadok, a mighty young man of valor, with 22 commanders from his own family.

verses.kjv: And of the children of Benjamin, the kindred of Saul, three thousand: for hitherto the greatest part of them had kept the ward of the house of Saul.
verses.bsb: From Benjamin, the kinsmen of Saul: 3,000, most of whom had remained loyal to the house of Saul up to that time.

verses.kjv: And of the children of Ephraim twenty thousand and eight hundred, mighty men of valour, famous throughout the house of their fathers.
verses.bsb: From Ephraim: 20,800 mighty men of valor, famous among their own clans.

verses.kjv: And of the half tribe of Manasseh eighteen thousand, which were expressed by name, to come and make David king.
verses.bsb: From the half-tribe of Manasseh: 18,000 designated by name to come and make David king.

verses.kjv: And of the children of Issachar, which were men that had understanding of the times, to know what Israel ought to do; the heads of them were two hundred; and all their brethren were at their commandment.
verses.bsb: From Issachar, men who understood the times and knew what Israel should do: 200 chiefs with all their kinsmen at their command.

verses.kjv: Of Zebulun, such as went forth to battle, expert in war, with all instruments of war, fifty thousand, which could keep rank: they were not of double heart.
verses.bsb: From Zebulun: 50,000 fit for service, trained for battle with all kinds of weapons of war, who with one purpose were devoted to David.

verses.kjv: And of Naphtali a thousand captains, and with them with shield and spear thirty and seven thousand.
verses.bsb: From Naphtali: 1,000 commanders, accompanied by 37,000 men with shield and spear.

verses.kjv: And of the Danites expert in war twenty and eight thousand and six hundred.
verses.bsb: From Dan: 28,600 prepared for battle.

verses.kjv: And of Asher, such as went forth to battle, expert in war, forty thousand.
verses.bsb: From Asher: 40,000 fit for service, prepared for battle.

verses.kjv: And on the other side of Jordan, of the Reubenites, and the Gadites, and of the half tribe of Manasseh, with all manner of instruments of war for the battle, an hundred and twenty thousand.
verses.bsb: And from east of the Jordan, from Reuben, Gad, and the half-tribe of Manasseh there: 120,000 armed with every kind of weapon of war.

verses.kjv: All these men of war, that could keep rank, came with a perfect heart to Hebron, to make David king over all Israel: and all the rest also of Israel were of one heart to make David king.
verses.bsb: All these men of war, arrayed for battle, came to Hebron fully determined to make David king over all Israel. And all the rest of the Israelites were of one mind to make David king.

verses.kjv: And there they were with David three days, eating and drinking: for their brethren had prepared for them.
verses.bsb: They spent three days there eating and drinking with David, for their relatives had provided for them.

verses.kjv: Moreover they that were nigh them, even unto Issachar and Zebulun and Naphtali, brought bread on asses, and on camels, and on mules, and on oxen, and meat, meal, cakes of figs, and bunches of raisins, and wine, and oil, and oxen, and sheep abundantly: for there was joy in Israel.
verses.bsb: And their neighbors from as far away as Issachar, Zebulun, and Naphtali came bringing food on donkeys, camels, mules, and oxen—abundant supplies of flour, fig cakes and raisin cakes, wine and oil, oxen and sheep. Indeed, there was joy in Israel.

verses.kjv: AND David consulted with the captains of thousands and hundreds, and with every leader.
verses.bsb: Then David conferred with all his leaders, the commanders of hundreds and of thousands.

verses.kjv: And David said unto all the congregation of Israel, If it seem good unto you, and that it be of the Lord our God, let us send abroad unto our brethren every where, that are left in all the land of Israel, and with them also to the priests and Levites which are in their cities and suburbs, that they may gather themselves unto us:
verses.bsb: And he said to the whole assembly of Israel, “If it seems good to you, and if this is of the LORD our God, let us send word far and wide to the rest of our brothers in all the land of Israel, and also to the priests and Levites in their cities and pasturelands, so that they may join us.

verses.kjv: And let us bring again the ark of our God to us: for we inquired not at it in the days of Saul.
verses.bsb: Then let us bring back the ark of our God, for we did not inquire of Him in the days of Saul.”

verses.kjv: And all the congregation said that they would do so: for the thing was right in the eyes of all the people.
verses.bsb: And because this proposal seemed right to all the people, the whole assembly agreed to it.

verses.kjv: So David gathered all Israel together, from Shihor of Egypt even unto the entering of Hemath, to bring the ark of God from Kirjath-jearim.
verses.bsb: So David assembled all Israel, from the River Shihor in Egypt to Lebo-hamath, to bring the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim.

verses.kjv: And David went up, and all Israel, to Baalah, that is, to Kirjath-jearim, which belonged to Judah, to bring up thence the ark of God the Lord, that dwelleth between the cherubims, whose name is called on it.
verses.bsb: David and all Israel went up to Baalah of Judah (that is, Kiriath-jearim) to bring up from there the ark of God the LORD, who is enthroned between the cherubim—the ark that is called by the Name.

verses.kjv: And they carried the ark of God in a new cart out of the house of Abinadab: and Uzza and Ahio drave the cart.
verses.bsb: So they carried the ark of God from the house of Abinadab on a new cart, with Uzzah and Ahio guiding the cart.

verses.kjv: And David and all Israel played before God with all their might, and with singing, and with harps, and with psalteries, and with timbrels, and with cymbals, and with trumpets.
verses.bsb: David and all the Israelites were celebrating before God with all their might, with songs and on harps and lyres, with tambourines, cymbals, and trumpets.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when they came unto the threshingfloor of Chidon, Uzza put forth his hand to hold the ark; for the oxen stumbled.
verses.bsb: When they came to the threshing floor of Chidon, Uzzah reached out and took hold of the ark, because the oxen had stumbled.

verses.kjv: And the anger of the Lord was kindled against Uzza, and he smote him, because he put his hand to the ark: and there he died before God.
verses.bsb: And the anger of the LORD burned against Uzzah, and He struck him down because he had put his hand on the ark. So he died there before God.

verses.kjv: And David was displeased, because the Lord had made a breach upon Uzza: wherefore that place is called Perez-uzza to this day.
verses.bsb: Then David became angry because the LORD had burst forth against Uzzah; so he named that place Perez-uzzah, as it is called to this day.

verses.kjv: And David was afraid of God that day, saying, How shall I bring the ark of God home to me?
verses.bsb: That day David feared God and asked, “How can I ever bring the ark of God to me?”

verses.kjv: So David brought not the ark home to himself to the city of David, but carried it aside into the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.
verses.bsb: So he did not move the ark with him to the City of David; instead, he took it aside to the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.

verses.kjv: And the ark of God remained with the family of Obed-edom in his house three months. And the Lord blessed the house of Obed-edom, and all that he had.
verses.bsb: Thus the ark of God remained with the family of Obed-edom in his house for three months, and the LORD blessed his household and everything he owned.

verses.kjv: NOW Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers to David, and timber of cedars, with masons and carpenters, to build him an house.
verses.bsb: Now Hiram king of Tyre sent envoys to David, along with cedar logs, stonemasons, and carpenters, to build a palace for him.

verses.kjv: And David perceived that the Lord had confirmed him king over Israel, for his kingdom was lifted up on high, because of his people Israel.
verses.bsb: And David realized that the LORD had established him as king over Israel and had highly exalted his kingdom for the sake of His people Israel.

verses.kjv: ¶ And David took more wives at Jerusalem: and David begat more sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And David took more wives in Jerusalem and became the father of more sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: Now these are the names of his children which he had in Jerusalem; Shammua, and Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon,
verses.bsb: These are the names of the children born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,

verses.kjv: And Ibhar, and Elishua, and Elpalet,
verses.bsb: Ibhar, Elishua, Elpelet,

verses.kjv: And Nogah, and Nepheg, and Japhia,
verses.bsb: Nogah, Nepheg, Japhia,

verses.kjv: And Elishama, and Beeliada, and Eliphalet.
verses.bsb: Elishama, Beeliada, and Eliphelet.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when the Philistines heard that David was anointed king over all Israel, all the Philistines went up to seek David. And David heard of it, and went out against them.
verses.bsb: When the Philistines heard that David had been anointed king over all Israel, they all went in search of him; but David learned of this and went out to face them.

verses.kjv: And the Philistines came and spread themselves in the valley of Rephaim.
verses.bsb: Now the Philistines had come and raided the Valley of Rephaim.

verses.kjv: And David inquired of God, saying, Shall I go up against the Philistines? and wilt thou deliver them into mine hand? And the Lord said unto him, Go up; for I will deliver them into thine hand.
verses.bsb: So David inquired of God, “Should I go up against the Philistines? Will You deliver them into my hand?”  “Go,” replied the LORD, “for I will deliver them into your hand.”

verses.kjv: So they came up to Baal-perazim; and David smote them there. Then David said, God hath broken in upon mine enemies by mine hand like the breaking forth of waters: therefore they called the name of that place Baal-perazim.
verses.bsb: So David and his men went up to Baal-perazim, where he defeated the Philistines and said, “Like a bursting flood, God has burst out against my enemies by my hand.” So they called that place Baal-perazim.

verses.kjv: And when they had left their gods there, David gave a commandment, and they were burned with fire.
verses.bsb: There the Philistines abandoned their gods, and David ordered that they be burned in the fire.

verses.kjv: And the Philistines yet again spread themselves abroad in the valley.
verses.bsb: Once again the Philistines raided the valley.

verses.kjv: Therefore David inquired again of God; and God said unto him, Go not up after them; turn away from them, and come upon them over against the mulberry trees.
verses.bsb: So David again inquired of God, who answered him, “Do not march up after them, but circle around them and attack them in front of the balsam trees.

verses.kjv: And it shall be, when thou shalt hear a sound of going in the tops of the mulberry trees, that then thou shalt go out to battle: for God is gone forth before thee to smite the host of the Philistines.
verses.bsb: As soon as you hear the sound of marching in the tops of the balsam trees, move out to battle, because this will mean that God has marched out before you to strike the camp of the Philistines.”

verses.kjv: David therefore did as God commanded him: and they smote the host of the Philistines from Gibeon even to Gazer.
verses.bsb: So David did as God had commanded him, and they struck down the army of the Philistines all the way from Gibeon to Gezer.

verses.kjv: And the fame of David went out into all lands; and the Lord brought the fear of him upon all nations.
verses.bsb: And David’s fame went out into every land, and the LORD caused all nations to fear him.

verses.kjv: AND David made him houses in the city of David, and prepared a place for the ark of God, and pitched for it a tent.
verses.bsb: David constructed buildings for himself in the City of David, and he prepared a place for the ark of God and pitched a tent for it.

verses.kjv: Then David said, None ought to carry the ark of God but the Levites: for them hath the Lord chosen to carry the ark of God, and to minister unto him for ever.
verses.bsb: Then David said, “No one but the Levites may carry the ark of God, because the LORD has chosen them to carry the ark of the LORD and to minister before Him forever.”

verses.kjv: And David gathered all Israel together to Jerusalem, to bring up the ark of the Lord unto his place, which he had prepared for it.
verses.bsb: And David assembled all Israel in Jerusalem to bring up the ark of the LORD to the place he had prepared for it.

verses.kjv: And David assembled the children of Aaron, and the Levites:
verses.bsb: Then he gathered together the descendants of Aaron and the Levites:

verses.kjv: Of the sons of Kohath; Uriel the chief, and his brethren an hundred and twenty:
verses.bsb: From the Kohathites, Uriel the chief and 120 of his relatives;

verses.kjv: Of the sons of Merari; Asaiah the chief, and his brethren two hundred and twenty:
verses.bsb: from the Merarites, Asaiah the chief and 220 of his relatives;

verses.kjv: Of the sons of Gershom; Joel the chief, and his brethren an hundred and thirty:
verses.bsb: from the Gershomites, Joel the chief and 130 of his relatives;

verses.kjv: Of the sons of Elizaphan; Shemaiah the chief, and his brethren two hundred:
verses.bsb: from the Elizaphanites, Shemaiah the chief and 200 of his relatives;

verses.kjv: Of the sons of Hebron; Eliel the chief, and his brethren fourscore:
verses.bsb: from the Hebronites, Eliel the chief and 80 of his relatives;

verses.kjv: Of the sons of Uzziel; Amminadab the chief, and his brethren an hundred and twelve.
verses.bsb: and from the Uzzielites, Amminadab the chief and 112 of his relatives.

verses.kjv: And David called for Zadok and Abiathar the priests, and for the Levites, for Uriel, Asaiah, and Joel, Shemaiah, and Eliel, and Amminadab,
verses.bsb: David summoned the priests Zadok and Abiathar and the Levites Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Shemaiah, Eliel, and Amminadab.

verses.kjv: And said unto them, Ye are the chief of the fathers of the Levites: sanctify yourselves, both ye and your brethren, that ye may bring up the ark of the Lord God of Israel unto the place that I have prepared for it.
verses.bsb: And he said to them, “You are the heads of the Levitical families. You and your relatives must consecrate yourselves so that you may bring the ark of the LORD, the God of Israel, to the place I have prepared for it.

verses.kjv: For because ye did it not at the first, the Lord our God made a breach upon us, for that we sought him not after the due order.
verses.bsb: It was because you Levites were not with us the first time that the LORD our God burst forth in anger against us. For we did not consult Him about the proper order.”

verses.kjv: So the priests and the Levites sanctified themselves to bring up the ark of the Lord God of Israel.
verses.bsb: So the priests and Levites consecrated themselves to bring up the ark of the LORD, the God of Israel.

verses.kjv: And the children of the Levites bare the ark of God upon their shoulders with the staves thereon, as Moses commanded according to the word of the Lord.
verses.bsb: And the Levites carried the ark of God on their shoulders with the poles, as Moses had commanded in accordance with the word of the LORD.

verses.kjv: And David spake to the chief of the Levites to appoint their brethren to be the singers with instruments of musick, psalteries and harps and cymbals, sounding, by lifting up the voice with joy.
verses.bsb: David also told the leaders of the Levites to appoint their relatives as singers to lift up their voices with joy, accompanied by musical instruments—harps, lyres, and cymbals.

verses.kjv: So the Levites appointed Heman the son of Joel; and of his brethren, Asaph the son of Berechiah; and of the sons of Merari their brethren, Ethan the son of Kushaiah;
verses.bsb: So the Levites appointed Heman son of Joel; from his brothers, Asaph son of Berechiah; from their brothers the Merarites, Ethan son of Kushaiah;

verses.kjv: And with them their brethren of the second degree, Zechariah, Ben, and Jaaziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Unni, Eliab, and Benaiah, and Maaseiah, and Mattithiah, and Elipheleh, and Mikneiah, and Obed-edom, and Jeiel, the porters.
verses.bsb: and with them their brothers next in rank: Zechariah, Jaaziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, and the gatekeepers Obed-edom and Jeiel.

verses.kjv: So the singers, Heman, Asaph, and Ethan, were appointed to sound with cymbals of brass;
verses.bsb: The musicians Heman, Asaph, and Ethan were to sound the bronze cymbals.

verses.kjv: And Zechariah, and Aziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Unni, and Eliab, and Maaseiah, and Benaiah, with psalteries on Alamoth;
verses.bsb: Zechariah, Aziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Maaseiah, and Benaiah were to play the harps according to Alamoth.

verses.kjv: And Mattithiah, and Elipheleh, and Mikneiah, and Obed-edom, and Jeiel, and Azaziah, with harps on the Sheminith to excel.
verses.bsb: And Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, Obed-edom, Jeiel, and Azaziah were to lead the music with lyres according to Sheminith.

verses.kjv: And Chenaniah, chief of the Levites, was for song: he instructed about the song, because he was skilful.
verses.bsb: Chenaniah the head Levite was the director of the music because he was highly skilled.

verses.kjv: And Berechiah and Elkanah were doorkeepers for the ark.
verses.bsb: Berechiah and Elkanah were to be guardians of the ark.

verses.kjv: And Shebaniah, and Jehoshaphat, and Nethaneel, and Amasai, and Zechariah, and Benaiah, and Eliezer, the priests, did blow with the trumpets before the ark of God: and Obed-edom and Jehiah were doorkeepers for the ark.
verses.bsb: Shebaniah, Joshaphat, Nethanel, Amasai, Zechariah, Benaiah, and Eliezer—the priests—were to blow the trumpets before the ark of God. Obed-edom and Jehiah were also to be guardians of the ark.

verses.kjv: ¶ So David, and the elders of Israel, and the captains over thousands, went to bring up the ark of the covenant of the Lord out of the house of Obed-edom with joy.
verses.bsb: So David, the elders of Israel, and the commanders of thousands went with rejoicing to bring the ark of the covenant of the LORD from the house of Obed-edom.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when God helped the Levites that bare the ark of the covenant of the Lord, that they offered seven bullocks and seven rams.
verses.bsb: And because God helped the Levites who were carrying the ark of the covenant of the LORD, they sacrificed seven bulls and seven rams.

verses.kjv: And David was clothed with a robe of fine linen, and all the Levites that bare the ark, and the singers, and Chenaniah the master of the song with the singers: David also had upon him an ephod of linen.
verses.bsb: Now David was dressed in a robe of fine linen, as were all the Levites who were carrying the ark, as well as the singers and Chenaniah, the director of music for the singers. David also wore a linen ephod.

verses.kjv: Thus all Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of the Lord with shouting, and with sound of the cornet, and with trumpets, and with cymbals, making a noise with psalteries and harps.
verses.bsb: So all Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of the LORD with shouting, with the sounding of rams’ horns and trumpets, and with cymbals and the music of harps and lyres.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, as the ark of the covenant of the Lord came to the city of David, that Michal the daughter of Saul looking out at a window saw king David dancing and playing: and she despised him in her heart.
verses.bsb: As the ark of the covenant of the LORD was entering the City of David, Saul’s daughter Michal looked down from a window and saw King David dancing and celebrating, and she despised him in her heart.

verses.kjv: SO they brought the ark of God, and set it in the midst of the tent that David had pitched for it: and they offered burnt sacrifices and peace offerings before God.
verses.bsb: So they brought the ark of God and placed it inside the tent that David had pitched for it. And they presented burnt offerings and peace offerings before God.

verses.kjv: And when David had made an end of offering the burnt offerings and the peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: When David had finished sacrificing the burnt offerings and peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the LORD.

verses.kjv: And he dealt to every one of Israel, both man and woman, to every one a loaf of bread, and a good piece of flesh, and a flagon of wine.
verses.bsb: Then he distributed to every man and woman of Israel a loaf of bread, a date cake, and a raisin cake.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he appointed certain of the Levites to minister before the ark of the Lord, and to record, and to thank and praise the Lord God of Israel:
verses.bsb: David appointed some of the Levites to minister before the ark of the LORD, to celebrate, to give thanks, and to praise the LORD, the God of Israel.

verses.kjv: Asaph the chief, and next to him Zechariah, Jeiel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Mattithiah, and Eliab, and Benaiah, and Obed-edom: and Jeiel with psalteries and with harps; but Asaph made a sound with cymbals;
verses.bsb: Asaph was the chief, Zechariah was second, then Jeiel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Mattithiah, Eliab, Benaiah, Obed-edom, and Jeiel. They were to play the harps and lyres, while Asaph sounded the cymbals

verses.kjv: Benaiah also and Jahaziel the priests with trumpets continually before the ark of the covenant of God.
verses.bsb: and the priests Benaiah and Jahaziel blew the trumpets regularly before the ark of the covenant of God.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then on that day David delivered first this psalm to thank the Lord into the hand of Asaph and his brethren.
verses.bsb: On that day David first committed to Asaph and his brothers this song of thanksgiving to the LORD:

verses.kjv: Give thanks unto the Lord, call upon his name, make known his deeds among the people.
verses.bsb: “Give thanks to the LORD; call upon His name; make known His deeds among the nations.

verses.kjv: Sing unto him, sing psalms unto him, talk ye of all his wondrous works.
verses.bsb: Sing to Him, sing praises to Him; tell of all His wonders.

verses.kjv: Glory ye in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek the Lord.
verses.bsb: Glory in His holy name; let the hearts of those who seek the LORD rejoice.

verses.kjv: Seek the Lord and his strength, seek his face continually.
verses.bsb: Seek out the LORD and His strength; seek His face always.

verses.kjv: Remember his marvellous works that he hath done, his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth;
verses.bsb: Remember the wonders He has done, His marvels, and the judgments He has pronounced,

verses.kjv: O ye seed of Israel his servant, ye children of Jacob, his chosen ones.
verses.bsb: O offspring of His servant Israel, O sons of Jacob, His chosen ones.

verses.kjv: He is the Lord our God; his judgments are in all the earth.
verses.bsb: He is the LORD our God; His judgments carry throughout the earth.

verses.kjv: Be ye mindful always of his covenant; the word which he commanded to a thousand generations;
verses.bsb: Remember His covenant forever, the word He ordained for a thousand generations—

verses.kjv: Even of the covenant which he made with Abraham, and of his oath unto Isaac;
verses.bsb: the covenant He made with Abraham, and the oath He swore to Isaac.

verses.kjv: And hath confirmed the same to Jacob for a law, and to Israel for an everlasting covenant,
verses.bsb: He confirmed it to Jacob as a decree, to Israel as an everlasting covenant:

verses.kjv: Saying, Unto thee will I give the land of Canaan, the lot of your inheritance;
verses.bsb: ‘I will give you the land of Canaan as the portion of your inheritance.’

verses.kjv: When ye were but few, even a few, and strangers in it.
verses.bsb: When they were few in number, few indeed, and strangers in the land,

verses.kjv: And when they went from nation to nation, and from one kingdom to another people;
verses.bsb: they wandered from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another.

verses.kjv: He suffered no man to do them wrong: yea, he reproved kings for their sakes,
verses.bsb: He let no man oppress them; He rebuked kings on their behalf:

verses.kjv: Saying, Touch not mine anointed, and do my prophets no harm.
verses.bsb: ‘Do not touch My anointed ones! Do no harm to My prophets!’

verses.kjv: Sing unto the Lord, all the earth; shew forth from day to day his salvation.
verses.bsb: Sing to the LORD, all the earth. Proclaim His salvation day after day.

verses.kjv: Declare his glory among the heathen; his marvellous works among all nations.
verses.bsb: Declare His glory among the nations, His wonderful deeds among all peoples.

verses.kjv: For great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised: he also is to be feared above all gods.
verses.bsb: For great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised; He is to be feared above all gods.

verses.kjv: For all the gods of the people are idols: but the Lord made the heavens.
verses.bsb: For all the gods of the nations are idols, but it is the LORD who made the heavens.

verses.kjv: Glory and honour are in his presence; strength and gladness are in his place.
verses.bsb: Splendor and majesty are before Him; strength and joy fill His dwelling.

verses.kjv: Give unto the Lord, ye kindreds of the people, give unto the Lord glory and strength.
verses.bsb: Ascribe to the LORD, O families of the nations, ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.

verses.kjv: Give unto the Lord the glory due unto his name: bring an offering, and come before him: worship the Lord in the beauty of holiness.
verses.bsb: Ascribe to the LORD the glory due His name; bring an offering and come before Him. Worship the LORD in the splendor of His holiness;

verses.kjv: Fear before him, all the earth: the world also shall be stable, that it be not moved.
verses.bsb: tremble before Him, all the earth. The world is firmly established; it cannot be moved.

verses.kjv: Let the heavens be glad, and let the earth rejoice: and let men say among the nations, The Lord reigneth.
verses.bsb: Let the heavens be glad, and the earth rejoice. Let them say among the nations, ‘The LORD reigns!’

verses.kjv: Let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof: let the fields rejoice, and all that is therein.
verses.bsb: Let the sea resound, and all that fills it; let the fields exult, and all that is in them.

verses.kjv: Then shall the trees of the wood sing out at the presence of the Lord, because he cometh to judge the earth.
verses.bsb: Then the trees of the forest will sing for joy before the LORD, for He is coming to judge the earth.

verses.kjv: O give thanks unto the Lord; for he is good; for his mercy endureth for ever.
verses.bsb: Give thanks to the LORD, for He is good; His loving devotion endures forever.

verses.kjv: And say ye, Save us, O God of our salvation, and gather us together, and deliver us from the heathen, that we may give thanks to thy holy name, and glory in thy praise.
verses.bsb: Then cry out: ‘Save us, O God of our salvation; gather and deliver us from the nations, that we may give thanks to Your holy name, that we may glory in Your praise.’

verses.kjv: Blessed be the Lord God of Israel for ever and ever. And all the people said, Amen, and praised the Lord.
verses.bsb: Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting.”   Then all the people said, “Amen!” and “Praise the LORD!”

verses.kjv: ¶ So he left there before the ark of the covenant of the Lord Asaph and his brethren, to minister before the ark continually, as every day’s work required:
verses.bsb: So David left Asaph and his brothers there before the ark of the covenant of the LORD, to minister there regularly according to the daily requirements,

verses.kjv: And Obed-edom with their brethren, threescore and eight; Obed-edom also the son of Jeduthun and Hosah to be porters:
verses.bsb: along with Obed-edom and his sixty-eight relatives. Obed-edom son of Jeduthun, and also Hosah, were to be gatekeepers.

verses.kjv: And Zadok the priest, and his brethren the priests, before the tabernacle of the Lord in the high place that was at Gibeon,
verses.bsb: And David left Zadok the priest and his fellow priests before the tabernacle of the LORD at the high place in Gibeon

verses.kjv: To offer burnt offerings unto the Lord upon the altar of the burnt offering continually morning and evening, and to do according to all that is written in the law of the Lord, which he commanded Israel;
verses.bsb: to regularly present burnt offerings to the LORD on the altar of burnt offerings, morning and evening, according to all that was written in the Law of the LORD, which He had commanded Israel to keep.

verses.kjv: And with them Heman and Jeduthun, and the rest that were chosen, who were expressed by name, to give thanks to the Lord, because his mercy endureth for ever;
verses.bsb: With them were Heman, Jeduthun, and the rest of those chosen and designated by name to give thanks to the LORD, for “His loving devotion endures forever.”

verses.kjv: And with them Heman and Jeduthun with trumpets and cymbals for those that should make a sound, and with musical instruments of God. And the sons of Jeduthun were porters.
verses.bsb: Heman and Jeduthun had with them trumpets and cymbals for the music and instruments for the songs of God. And the sons of Jeduthun were stationed at the gate.

verses.kjv: And all the people departed every man to his house: and David returned to bless his house.
verses.bsb: Then all the people departed for their homes, and David returned home to bless his household.

verses.kjv: NOW it came to pass, as David sat in his house, that David said to Nathan the prophet, Lo, I dwell in an house of cedars, but the ark of the covenant of the Lord remaineth under curtains.
verses.bsb: After David had settled into his palace, he said to Nathan the prophet, “Here I am, living in a house of cedar, while the ark of the covenant of the LORD is under a tent.”

verses.kjv: Then Nathan said unto David, Do all that is in thine heart; for God is with thee.
verses.bsb: And Nathan replied to David, “Do all that is in your heart, for God is with you.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass the same night, that the word of God came to Nathan, saying,
verses.bsb: But that night the word of God came to Nathan, saying,

verses.kjv: Go and tell David my servant, Thus saith the Lord, Thou shalt not build me an house to dwell in:
verses.bsb: “Go and tell My servant David that this is what the LORD says: You are not the one to build Me a house in which to dwell.

verses.kjv: For I have not dwelt in an house since the day that I brought up Israel unto this day; but have gone from tent to tent, and from one tabernacle to another.
verses.bsb: For I have not dwelt in a house from the day I brought Israel up out of Egypt until this day, but I have moved from tent to tent and dwelling to dwelling.

verses.kjv: Wheresoever I have walked with all Israel, spake I a word to any of the judges of Israel, whom I commanded to feed my people, saying, Why have ye not built me an house of cedars?
verses.bsb: In all My journeys with all the Israelites, have I ever asked any of the leaders I appointed to shepherd My people, ‘Why haven’t you built Me a house of cedar?’

verses.kjv: Now therefore thus shalt thou say unto my servant David, Thus saith the Lord of hosts, I took thee from the sheepcote, even from following the sheep, that thou shouldest be ruler over my people Israel:
verses.bsb: Now then, you are to tell My servant David that this is what the LORD of Hosts says: I took you from the pasture, from following the flock, to be the ruler over My people Israel.

verses.kjv: And I have been with thee whithersoever thou hast walked, and have cut off all thine enemies from before thee, and have made thee a name like the name of the great men that are in the earth.
verses.bsb: I have been with you wherever you have gone, and I have cut off all your enemies from before you. Now I will make for you a name like the greatest in the land.

verses.kjv: Also I will ordain a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, and they shall dwell in their place, and shall be moved no more; neither shall the children of wickedness waste them any more, as at the beginning,
verses.bsb: And I will provide a place for My people Israel and will plant them so that they may dwell in a place of their own and be disturbed no more. No longer will the sons of wickedness oppress them as they did at the beginning

verses.kjv: And since the time that I commanded judges to be over my people Israel. Moreover I will subdue all thine enemies. Furthermore I tell thee that the Lord will build thee an house.
verses.bsb: and have done since the day I appointed judges over My people Israel. And I will subdue all your enemies.  Moreover, I declare to you that the LORD will build a house for you.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it shall come to pass, when thy days be expired that thou must go to be with thy fathers, that I will raise up thy seed after thee, which shall be of thy sons; and I will establish his kingdom.
verses.bsb: And when your days are fulfilled and you go to be with your fathers, I will raise up your descendant after you, one of your own sons, and I will establish his kingdom.

verses.kjv: He shall build me an house, and I will stablish his throne for ever.
verses.bsb: He will build a house for Me, and I will establish his throne forever.

verses.kjv: I will be his father, and he shall be my son: and I will not take my mercy away from him, as I took it from him that was before thee:
verses.bsb: I will be his Father, and he will be My son. And I will never remove My loving devotion from him as I removed it from your predecessor.

verses.kjv: But I will settle him in mine house and in my kingdom for ever: and his throne shall be established for evermore.
verses.bsb: But I will set him over My house and My kingdom forever, and his throne will be established forever.”

verses.kjv: According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so did Nathan speak unto David.
verses.bsb: So Nathan relayed to David all the words of this entire vision.

verses.kjv: ¶ And David the king came and sat before the Lord, and said, Who am I, O Lord God, and what is mine house, that thou hast brought me hitherto?
verses.bsb: Then King David went in, sat before the LORD, and said, “Who am I, O LORD God, and what is my house, that You have brought me this far?

verses.kjv: And yet this was a small thing in thine eyes, O God; for thou hast also spoken of thy servant’s house for a great while to come, and hast regarded me according to the estate of a man of high degree, O Lord God.
verses.bsb: And as if this was a small thing in Your eyes, O God, You have spoken about the future of the house of Your servant and have regarded me as a man of great distinction, O LORD God.

verses.kjv: What can David speak more to thee for the honour of thy servant? for thou knowest thy servant.
verses.bsb: What more can David say to You for so honoring Your servant? For You know Your servant,

verses.kjv: O Lord, for thy servant’s sake, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all this greatness, in making known all these great things.
verses.bsb: O LORD. For the sake of Your servant and according to Your own heart, You have accomplished this great thing and revealed all Your greatness.

verses.kjv: O Lord, there is none like thee, neither is there any God beside thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
verses.bsb: O LORD, there is none like You, and there is no God but You, according to everything we have heard with our own ears.

verses.kjv: And what one nation in the earth is like thy people Israel, whom God went to redeem to be his own people, to make thee a name of greatness and terribleness, by driving out nations from before thy people, whom thou hast redeemed out of Egypt?
verses.bsb: And who is like Your people Israel—the one nation on earth whom God went out to redeem as a people for Himself? You made a name for Yourself through great and awesome wonders by driving out nations from before Your people, whom You redeemed from Egypt.

verses.kjv: For thy people Israel didst thou make thine own people for ever; and thou, Lord, becamest their God.
verses.bsb: For You have made Your people Israel Your very own forever, and You, O LORD, have become their God.

verses.kjv: Therefore now, Lord, let the thing that thou hast spoken concerning thy servant and concerning his house be established for ever, and do as thou hast said.
verses.bsb: And now, O LORD, let the word You have spoken concerning Your servant and his house be established forever. Do as You have promised,

verses.kjv: Let it even be established, that thy name may be magnified for ever, saying, The Lord of hosts is the God of Israel, even a God to Israel: and let the house of David thy servant be established before thee.
verses.bsb: so that Your name will be established and magnified forever when it is said, ‘The LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel, is God over Israel.’ And may the house of Your servant David be established before You.

verses.kjv: For thou, O my God, hast told thy servant that thou wilt build him an house: therefore thy servant hath found in his heart to pray before thee.
verses.bsb: For You, my God, have revealed to Your servant that You will build a house for him. Therefore Your servant has found the courage to pray before You.

verses.kjv: And now, Lord, thou art God, and hast promised this goodness unto thy servant:
verses.bsb: And now, O LORD, You are God! And You have promised this goodness to Your servant.

verses.kjv: Now therefore let it please thee to bless the house of thy servant, that it may be before thee for ever: for thou blessest, O Lord, and it shall be blessed for ever.
verses.bsb: So now You have been pleased to bless the house of Your servant, that it may continue forever before You. For You, O LORD, have blessed it, and it will be blessed forever.”

verses.kjv: NOW after this it came to pass, that David smote the Philistines, and subdued them, and took Gath and her towns out of the hand of the Philistines.
verses.bsb: Some time later, David defeated the Philistines, subdued them, and took Gath and its villages from the hand of the Philistines.

verses.kjv: And he smote Moab; and the Moabites became David’s servants, and brought gifts.
verses.bsb: David also defeated the Moabites, and they became subject to David and brought him tribute.

verses.kjv: ¶ And David smote Hadarezer king of Zobah unto Hamath, as he went to stablish his dominion by the river Euphrates.
verses.bsb: As far as Hamath, David also defeated King Hadadezer of Zobah, who had marched out to establish his dominion along the Euphrates River.

verses.kjv: And David took from him a thousand chariots, and seven thousand horsemen, and twenty thousand footmen: David also houghed all the chariot horses, but reserved of them an hundred chariots.
verses.bsb: David captured from him a thousand chariots, seven thousand charioteers, and twenty thousand foot soldiers, and he hamstrung all the horses except a hundred he kept for the chariots.

verses.kjv: And when the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadarezer king of Zobah, David slew of the Syrians two and twenty thousand men.
verses.bsb: When the Arameans of Damascus came to help King Hadadezer of Zobah, David struck down twenty-two thousand men.

verses.kjv: Then David put garrisons in Syria-damascus; and the Syrians became David’s servants, and brought gifts. Thus the Lord preserved David whithersoever he went.
verses.bsb: Then he placed garrisons in Aram of Damascus, and the Arameans became subject to David and brought him tribute. So the LORD made David victorious wherever he went.

verses.kjv: And David took the shields of gold that were on the servants of Hadarezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: And David took the gold shields that belonged to the officers of Hadadezer and brought them to Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: Likewise from Tibhath, and from Chun, cities of Hadarezer, brought David very much brass, wherewith Solomon made the brasen sea, and the pillars, and the vessels of brass.
verses.bsb: And from Tibhath and Cun, cities of Hadadezer, David took a large amount of bronze, with which Solomon made the bronze Sea, the pillars, and various bronze articles.

verses.kjv: ¶ Now when Tou king of Hamath heard how David had smitten all the host of Hadarezer king of Zobah;
verses.bsb: When King Tou of Hamath heard that David had defeated the entire army of Hadadezer king of Zobah,

verses.kjv: He sent Hadoram his son to king David, to inquire of his welfare, and to congratulate him, because he had fought against Hadarezer, and smitten him; (for Hadarezer had war with Tou;) and with him all manner of vessels of gold and silver and brass.
verses.bsb: he sent his son Hadoram to greet King David and bless him for fighting and defeating Hadadezer, who had been at war with Tou. Hadoram brought all kinds of articles of gold and silver and bronze,

verses.kjv: ¶ Them also king David dedicated unto the Lord, with the silver and the gold that he brought from all these nations; from Edom, and from Moab, and from the children of Ammon, and from the Philistines, and from Amalek.
verses.bsb: and King David dedicated these to the LORD, along with the silver and gold he had carried off from all these nations—from Edom and Moab, and from the Ammonites, Philistines, and Amalekites.

verses.kjv: Moreover Abishai the son of Zeruiah slew of the Edomites in the valley of salt eighteen thousand.
verses.bsb: Moreover, Abishai son of Zeruiah struck down eighteen thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he put garrisons in Edom; and all the Edomites became David’s servants. Thus the Lord preserved David whithersoever he went.
verses.bsb: He placed garrisons in Edom, and all the Edomites were subject to David. So the LORD made David victorious wherever he went.

verses.kjv: ¶ So David reigned over all Israel, and executed judgment and justice among all his people.
verses.bsb: Thus David reigned over all Israel and administered justice and righteousness for all his people:

verses.kjv: And Joab the son of Zeruiah was over the host; and Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud, recorder.
verses.bsb: Joab son of Zeruiah was over the army;   Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was the recorder;

verses.kjv: And Zadok the son of Ahitub, and Abimelech the son of Abiathar, were the priests; and Shavsha was scribe;
verses.bsb: Zadok son of Ahitub and Ahimelech son of Abiathar were priests;   Shavsha was the scribe;

verses.kjv: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and the sons of David were chief about the king.
verses.bsb: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and Pelethites;   and David’s sons were chief officials at the king’s side.

verses.kjv: NOW it came to pass after this, that Nahash the king of the children of Ammon died, and his son reigned in his stead.
verses.bsb: Some time later, Nahash king of the Ammonites died and was succeeded by his son.

verses.kjv: And David said, I will shew kindness unto Hanun the son of Nahash, because his father shewed kindness to me. And David sent messengers to comfort him concerning his father. So the servants of David came into the land of the children of Ammon to Hanun, to comfort him.
verses.bsb: And David said, “I will show kindness to Hanun son of Nahash, because his father showed kindness to me.”  So David sent messengers to console Hanun concerning his father. But when David’s servants arrived in the land of the Ammonites to console him,

verses.kjv: But the princes of the children of Ammon said to Hanun, Thinkest thou that David doth honour thy father, that he hath sent comforters unto thee? are not his servants come unto thee for to search, and to overthrow, and to spy out the land?
verses.bsb: the princes of the Ammonites said to Hanun, “Just because David has sent you comforters, do you really believe he is showing respect for your father? Have not his servants come to you to explore the land, spy it out, and overthrow it?”

verses.kjv: Wherefore Hanun took David’s servants, and shaved them, and cut off their garments in the midst hard by their buttocks, and sent them away.
verses.bsb: So Hanun took David’s servants, shaved their beards, cut off their garments at the hips, and sent them away.

verses.kjv: Then there went certain, and told David how the men were served. And he sent to meet them: for the men were greatly ashamed. And the king said, Tarry at Jericho until your beards be grown, and then return.
verses.bsb: When someone came and told David about his men, he sent messengers to meet them, since the men had been thoroughly humiliated. The king told them, “Stay in Jericho until your beards have grown back, and then return.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when the children of Ammon saw that they had made themselves odious to David, Hanun and the children of Ammon sent a thousand talents of silver to hire them chariots and horsemen out of Mesopotamia, and out of Syria-maachah, and out of Zobah.
verses.bsb: When the Ammonites realized that they had become a stench to David, Hanun and the Ammonites sent a thousand talents of silver to hire for themselves chariots and horsemen from Aram-naharaim, Aram-maacah, and Zobah.

verses.kjv: So they hired thirty and two thousand chariots, and the king of Maachah and his people; who came and pitched before Medeba. And the children of Ammon gathered themselves together from their cities, and came to battle.
verses.bsb: So they hired for themselves thirty-two thousand chariots, as well as the king of Maacah with his troops, who came and camped near Medeba while the Ammonites came from their cities and marched out for battle.

verses.kjv: And when David heard of it, he sent Joab, and all the host of the mighty men.
verses.bsb: On hearing this, David sent Joab and the entire army of mighty men.

verses.kjv: And the children of Ammon came out, and put the battle in array before the gate of the city: and the kings that were come were by themselves in the field.
verses.bsb: The Ammonites marched out and arrayed themselves for battle at the entrance to the city, while the kings who had come stayed by themselves in the open country.

verses.kjv: Now when Joab saw that the battle was set against him before and behind, he chose out of all the choice of Israel, and put them in array against the Syrians.
verses.bsb: When Joab saw the battle lines before him and behind him, he selected some of the best men of Israel and arrayed them against the Arameans.

verses.kjv: And the rest of the people he delivered unto the hand of Abishai his brother, and they set themselves in array against the children of Ammon.
verses.bsb: And he placed the rest of the forces under the command of his brother Abishai, who arrayed them against the Ammonites.

verses.kjv: And he said, If the Syrians be too strong for me, then thou shalt help me: but if the children of Ammon be too strong for thee, then I will help thee.
verses.bsb: “If the Arameans are too strong for me,” said Joab, “then you will come to my rescue. And if the Ammonites are too strong for you, then I will come to your rescue.

verses.kjv: Be of good courage, and let us behave ourselves valiantly for our people, and for the cities of our God: and let the Lord do that which is good in his sight.
verses.bsb: Be strong and let us fight bravely for our people and for the cities of our God. May the LORD do what is good in His sight.”

verses.kjv: So Joab and the people that were with him drew nigh before the Syrians unto the battle; and they fled before him.
verses.bsb: So Joab and his troops advanced to fight the Arameans, who fled before him.

verses.kjv: And when the children of Ammon saw that the Syrians were fled, they likewise fled before Abishai his brother, and entered into the city. Then Joab came to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: When the Ammonites saw that the Arameans had fled, they too fled before Joab’s brother Abishai, and they entered the city. So Joab went back to Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when the Syrians saw that they were put to the worse before Israel, they sent messengers, and drew forth the Syrians that were beyond the river: and Shophach the captain of the host of Hadarezer went before them.
verses.bsb: When the Arameans saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they sent messengers to bring more Arameans from beyond the Euphrates, with Shophach the commander of Hadadezer’s army leading them.

verses.kjv: And it was told David; and he gathered all Israel, and passed over Jordan, and came upon them, and set the battle in array against them. So when David had put the battle in array against the Syrians, they fought with him.
verses.bsb: When this was reported to David, he gathered all Israel, crossed the Jordan, advanced toward the Arameans, and arrayed for battle against them. When David lined up to engage them in battle, they fought against him.

verses.kjv: But the Syrians fled before Israel; and David slew of the Syrians seven thousand men which fought in chariots, and forty thousand footmen, and killed Shophach the captain of the host.
verses.bsb: But the Arameans fled before Israel, and David killed seven thousand of their charioteers and forty thousand foot soldiers. He also killed Shophach the commander of their army.

verses.kjv: And when the servants of Hadarezer saw that they were put to the worse before Israel, they made peace with David, and became his servants: neither would the Syrians help the children of Ammon any more.
verses.bsb: When Hadadezer’s subjects saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they made peace with David and became subject to him. So the Arameans were unwilling to help the Ammonites anymore.

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, that after the year was expired, at the time that kings go out to battle, Joab led forth the power of the army, and wasted the country of the children of Ammon, and came and besieged Rabbah. But David tarried at Jerusalem. And Joab smote Rabbah, and destroyed it.
verses.bsb: In the spring, at the time when kings march out to war, Joab led out the army and ravaged the land of the Ammonites. He came to Rabbah and besieged it, but David remained in Jerusalem. And Joab attacked Rabbah and demolished it.
